title: Fix global mutable state in walk implementation
description:
**Location:** packages/core/src/parser.ts:388-399

**Problem:** Module-level mutable state (currentHandlers) is a major code smell that could cause bugs in concurrent/async scenarios and makes testing harder.

**Solution Options:**
1. Pass context through call stack (recommended)
2. Store handlers on node during parse
3. Use a Context class

**Priority:** Critical - This is the only serious code smell in the codebase

See: tmp/main/CODE_REVIEW.md for detailed solution examples

title: cm7 regression: forced-multiselect submit stopped wrapping to array; React gate was red but masked by tail-piped exit code
Tracer: present() stage + neutral FieldFacts + default widget rule (ADR 029)
description:
First vertical tracer for ADR 029, ONE PR, dual-period, commit-sequenced so the gate is green at every commit and main never carries the duplicated state.

Steps:
1. Add FieldFacts (path, label, description, required, primitive, valueShape:'scalar'|'array', format?, choices?, constraints, attrs, origin) to field nodes WITHOUT removing existing widget/parts.
2. Add Core present() + defaultPresentation (valueShape==='array' && choices -> multiselect; choices -> select; else input) + Core derivers for input/select/multiselect (extract from buildInputAttrs / select attrs in fieldNode.ts and createMultiselectFieldNode in arrayNode.ts). present() must preserve node identity for unchanged subtrees (structural sharing) so the React NodeRenderer memo-bail holds.
3. Wire useSchemaForm -> present().
4. Flip conformance to compare the post-present() tree.
5. Delete the parser's widget/parts logic; migrate/delete redundant parser unit tests into integration tests.

Golden integration test (product-owner style, assert DOM + submit, not internals): a schema with no multiselect hint + resolvePresentation forcing multiselect -> assert <select multiple> in the DOM and submit yields string[].

First tracer reuses the existing select dispatch (multiselect already renders via <select multiple>) so it does NOT include the field.control engine/adapter rewrite.

Deferred to later beads: bindRenders/extend custom-widget DX; unified field.control slot; origin rename (schema -> origin {source,schema:TOrigin}).
close_reason: Landed in PR #25 (rebase-merged to main: 70fbdba..7b09e0f)

title: Validator contract: purity invariant + transformed-data return (ADR 025)
description: Implement ADR 025. (1) Add optional `data?: unknown` to ValidationResult in packages/core/src/validation.ts. (2) AJV adapter: clone input before validate so coerceTypes never mutates the caller; return the coerced clone as result.data. (3) Zod adapter: return safeParse result.data on success. (4) validation-contract suite: add a purity assertion (input deep-equal before/after validate) and a transformed-data assertion (coerced '18'->18 reflected in result.data, input unchanged). (5) Delete the defensive JSON clone from the RHF recipe (App_12) now that the contract guarantees purity. Gate must stay green.
close_reason: Implemented ADR 025 on main (71a5918): ValidationResult<T> + data?, Validator purity invariant, AJV clones+returns coerced data (InferData<S>), Zod returns parsed output (TypeOf<T>), contract suite purity+no-aliasing assertions, per-adapter coercion tests, RHF recipe clone removed. Gate green.

title: Unify $ref resolution to walk allOf (single pass) + dedup JSON Pointer helpers
description: PR #8's mergeAllOf resolves $ref entries inside allOf only one level and duplicates decodeJsonPointerSegment/resolveJsonPointer from resolveRefs.ts. Teach resolveLocalRefs to walk allOf (or run a second resolution pass after merge) so chained $defs graphs inside allOf resolve, and extract shared JSON Pointer helpers. Add tests for items-level allOf and nested $ref/$defs graphs. Raised in PR #8 review.
notes: RHF spike added setNested/getNested dot-path helpers in the recipe (App_12) — more duplicated path manipulation alongside resolveRefs' JSON Pointer helpers, AJV's pointerToPath/join, and the engine's dense-array repathing. Reinforces consolidating into one shared core path-utils module. Pure get/set-by-dot-path is the reusable primitive; lift it here when a second non-recipe consumer appears (ADR 008).
close_reason: Merged PR #14 (6f2b41a): shared packages/core/src/jsonPointer.ts; resolveRefs.ts + ajvValidator.ts migrated; allOf-walk half deferred to jsonschema-form-0z9.

title: Evaluate Standard Schema alignment for the Validator seam
description: Standard Schema (standardschema.dev) standardizes a validator's issues as {message, path} (no keyword vocabulary). Evaluate aligning our Validator contract to the Standard Schema interface so Zod/Valibot/ArkType plug in natively. Relates to keyword-normalization bead jsonschema-form-1oz. Raised by maintainer on PR #2.
notes: ADR 026 (Proposed): speak Standard Schema at the boundary, don't replace the contract. Tracer in PR #15 — toStandardSchema/fromStandardSchema in core (inlined v1 types, zero-dep) + 10 tests, gate green. Awaiting review/merge.
close_reason: Merged PR #15 (c4f1baa, 0076ee0): ADR 026 — adapt Standard Schema at the boundary (toStandardSchema/fromStandardSchema in core), not adopt; reason is the dot-string node.path/issue.path that powers typed FieldPath. Follow-up: 1d3 (App_12 simplification).

title: React: JSX re-entry component layer (<Default of/> / <Children of/>) over callable handles
close_reason: Implemented in ADR 017: stable Default/Children components delegating to node callables (ADR 016); RenderNode -> (node, helpers); IOC injection + export; unified node/child/part, null-safe, typed parts. Gate green; conformance matches vanilla oracle; App_08 migrated + browser-verified.

title: React fold remounted nodes on real re-render (fresh-closure <node.Default/>)
close_reason: Fixed: React fold now renders by calling continuation handles (node.Default()/part.Default()) through stable module-level components (NodeRenderer/ArrayRoot/PartHost) + new renderPart engine seam. Real re-renders reconcile in place; no remount. ADR 016.

title: Densify array submission (sparse FormData -> dense array)
description: Array items keep stable, non-contiguous paths (e.g. contacts.1 survives after removing contacts.0) to preserve React identity/values (ADR 015). unflatten() currently yields a SPARSE array with holes. Decide + implement densification: compact in unflatten, or remap a display-index at submit. Crosses Core submit semantics - decide deliberately.
close_reason: Dense array submission solved via UI re-pathing (ADR 018), not a Core/unflatten change. React combine keys siblings by position (decoupling React identity from form path); ArrayRoot re-paths survivors densely event-time in the state updater. Removing the first of two items now submits a contiguous array { contacts: [{...}] } with no leading hole, value preserved in place. Verified by arrays.test.tsx (dense submit), render-counts.test.tsx (re-render contract), and live App_07 sanity.

title: Cross-framework HTML/CSS conformance suite (golden-DOM oracle)
description:
A framework-agnostic golden-DOM fixture set: (schema [+overrides]) -> normalized HTML. Each UI adapter (React today; vanilla/Vue next) renders the fixtures; Playwright normalizes the DOM (strip framework noise) and diffs against the golden. This becomes the gate that lets us swap UI frameworks safely while leaning on verification (ADR 009).

Reinforces ADR 012 §3 (Core owns the native HTML attrs) — identical output across frameworks is only possible if the structure/attrs are a shared contract. The eventual vanilla (no-framework) renderer likely doubles as the canonical oracle that produces the golden HTML. Open question (ADR 013): whether the default node-template *arrangement* should be a shared framework-agnostic descriptor vs. idiomatic-per-framework templates kept in sync by this suite.
close_reason: Built cross-framework conformance oracle: packages/react/src/conformance.test.tsx asserts react DOM ≡ vanilla string→DOM across all widgets, arrays, and renderNode overrides. Engine since extracted to Core (ADR 014); oracle proves equivalence.

title: Harden Core IR (ADR 012): widget-discriminated FieldNode/FieldParts + Core-owned typed HTML attrs
close_reason: Implemented in c4d51a9 on worktree-agentic-loop-experiment: widget-discriminated FieldNode/FieldParts + Core-owned HtmlInputAttrs/HtmlSelectAttrs (ADR 012), gate green

title: Golden scenarios green on the native zero-dep stack (Phase A north star)
description: Curate sanitized VNDLY-style golden scenarios; green at 3 altitudes (unit, component-integration, e2e) on Core + React + native FormData + bare UI + no validation. ADR 008/011.

title: Rebuild spike renderer as the real typed, perf-aware React renderer (ADR 010)
description: Promote examples/spikeRenderer.tsx into @jsonschema-form/react: resolver + enriched nodes (Default/Children/children/Child) + part-level parts overrides + scoped renderNode + <SchemaForm>{(root)=>...}. renderNode is structure-only and memoized; value reactivity lives at the leaves (form-state adapter), never re-walk on keystroke. Relates to epic jsonschema-form-3us.
close_reason: Delivered: typed continuation renderer in @jsonschema-form/react over Core's createContinuation (ADR 010/012/014). Enriched nodes (Default/Children/child), part-level overrides, scoped+memoized renderNode (structure-only, no keystroke re-walk), place-yourself children. Customization seam finalized as the compound RendererAdapter with a diagnostic floor; component is SchemaFields, chrome consumer-owned (ADR 013). Remaining renderer-adjacent work tracked separately (bi4 array UI).

title: Update outdated documentation (checklists, test counts, typos)
description:
**Quick Fixes Needed:**

1. README.md line 14: Fix typo 'Impecable' → 'Impeccable'
2. ARCHITECTURE.md line 154: Update checklist - parseSchema() is done!
3. ADR 002: Update test count from 54 to 57 tests
4. ARCHITECTURE.md: Update 'Next Steps' section with current status

**Estimated Time:** 30-60 minutes

**Files to Update:**
- README.md
- ARCHITECTURE.md
- architecture_records/002_parts_api_consolidation_and_validation_structure.md

title: Configure build process for core package
description:
**Current:** Build script just echoes placeholder message

**Need:**
- TypeScript compilation to dist/
- Declaration file generation (.d.ts)
- Proper entry points for ESM/CJS
- Bundle size optimization

**Recommended Tools:**
- tsup (preferred for libraries)
- rollup (more configuration)
- vite (modern alternative)

**Output:**
- dist/index.js (ESM)
- dist/index.cjs (CommonJS)
- dist/index.d.ts (TypeScript declarations)

**Required before v1.0 release**

title: Split parser.ts into multiple modules
description:
**Current State:** parser.ts is 426 lines and growing

**Proposed Structure:**
packages/core/src/parser/
  - index.ts (re-exports)
  - parseSchema.ts (main entry)
  - fieldNode.ts (createFieldNode + helpers)
  - groupNode.ts (createGroupNode + helpers)
  - parts.ts (part builders)
  - validation.ts (buildValidation, buildInputAttrs)
  - walk.ts (walkNode implementation)
  - serialize.ts (serializeNode, toJSON)

**Benefits:**
- Easier navigation
- Better separation of concerns
- Easier to test individual functions
- Clearer imports

title: Design and implement React layer (hooks-based, TanStack-style API)
Design and implement UISchema support for customization hints
close_reason: Closed per direction (2026-07-01): the library will NOT ship a concrete source format (uiSchema/inline). Customization is a source-agnostic presentation resolver over parsed nodes (p5l / ADR 022 layer 3 promoted). Consumers transform their source (inline x-*, sidecar uiSchema, DB blob) into our normalized presentation API.

title: Add array field support (repeating fields)
Add enum/select field type support
Add boolean field type support (checkboxes)
Uniform spreadable attrs per part + single neutral→framework translator
description: PR #36 review (comments 1,2,4). Generalize the l8j 'role into Core' + '{id, for?} spreadable label' moves into one principle: EVERY renderable part exposes its DOM attributes as one spreadable neutral attrs bag, and each adapter has exactly ONE neutral->framework translator (for->htmlFor, class->className, etc.) applied to any bag. No consumer hand-picks individual attributes. Concretely: (a) choicegroup control exposes wrapperAttrs { role, 'aria-labelledby' } to spread onto the wrapper <div> instead of consumers placing role/aria-labelledby by hand (demoting control.multiple to informational metadata); (b) a shared htmlAttrsToReactProps() helper in the React adapter; (c) audit every part (input/select/textarea/label/option/addButton/wrapper) for a consistent spreadable attrs surface. Likely warrants an ADR (public parts-contract change generalizing bd l8j). The minimal label {id, for?} slice already shipped in #36; this is the full generalization.

title: Root getAllFields() skips fields inside array subtrees
description: PR #33 (7mr) + groupNode.ts getAllFields recurse only into child GROUPS, not array item subtrees, so leaves inside dynamic arrays are omitted from the root's flat field list. Consumers needing a complete flat leaf list with arrays present must fall back to walk({ field }). Decide: make getAllFields include array-item leaves, add an opt-in ({ includeArrayItems: true }), or document the limitation. Pairs with the getField-through-arrays gap.

title: Root getField() cannot resolve paths through array indices
description: PR #33 (7mr) tests document that form.getField('org.department.members.0.name') from the ROOT group returns undefined — fields inside dynamic arrays are only reachable via relative getField on the item group or via walk(). Relative-path resolution is ADR-documented, but absolute lookup through numeric array indices is a real query gap for consumers that only hold the root node. Decide whether root getField should traverse array item children (e.g. 'members.0.name') and implement, or document the limitation explicitly in the API.

title: choicegroup caption a11y: aria-labelledby wrapper instead of label-for-first-option
description: PR #31 (cm7) review: for a radio/checkbox group the caption <label for> is retargeted at the FIRST option's id (present.ts deriveFieldParts). That programmatically associates the caption but has a surprising side effect: clicking the caption activates/selects the first radio. The canonical grouping patterns are (a) fieldset+legend, or (b) role=radiogroup|group on the wrapper + aria-labelledby pointing at the caption id. Proposal: extend the neutral a11y contract so a choicegroup emits a caption id + wrapper aria-labelledby (Core-derived, so React==vanilla), and drop the label-for retarget. This is an ADR-029 a11y-contract addition (label part gains an id; control/wrapper gains aria-labelledby), touching both adapters + oracles + conformance. Deferred out of cm7 to keep that PR to the widget/heuristic slice.

title: Implement the ADR 029 §5 unified field.control render slot
description: The single render slot that lets any widget (radio, checkbox-group, textarea, collapsed multiselect) render through one path instead of the closed input|select|multiselect dispatch in DefaultFieldRoot. Unblocks cm7 (radio/checkbox/textarea + heuristic), 9el (textarea), kon (tags/pills), and the RENDERING half of ADR 030 (fcj) collapsed containers. Core FieldNode union + engine FieldPartRenderers must open to a general control descriptor; React DefaultFieldRoot dispatches on it. This is the highest-leverage next step: it is the shared prerequisite for the whole widget-catalog expansion and for seeing ADR 030 collapse on screen.
close_reason: Merged as PR #30 — unified field.control render slot (ADR 029 §5) + textarea; clean break, gate green.

title: ADR: present()/renderNode layer boundary — widgets are (visual+value-contract) pairs
close_reason: ADR 031 merged (PR #29)

title: radio/textarea/checkbox widgets need the ADR 029 §5 field.control render slot
description:
Deferred from 4j1. Needs the ADR 029 §5 unified field.control render slot: today React's DefaultFieldRoot dispatches only input vs select, and the engine's FieldPartRenderers + Core's FieldNode union are closed to input|select|multiselect. Adding radio/checkbox-group/textarea requires that slot.

WIDGETS: radio (single-select, small N), checkbox group (multi-select, small N), textarea (long/multiline string).

DEFAULT-PRESENTATION HEURISTIC (option-count driven; agreed w/ Tim 2026-07-02) — all remain overridable by a consumer resolver (ADR 029):
- scalar + choices:   length <= 5 -> radio;   6-15 -> select;   16+ -> (future) combobox/typeahead.
- array + choices (multiple):   length <= 5 -> checkbox group;   else -> multiselect (<select multiple>).
- string w/ long/multiline hint (large maxLength or a textarea format) -> textarea (orthogonal to the count heuristic).
Put the threshold in ONE named constant (start at 5) so it is tunable.

WHY 5: convergent UX research. USWDS / VA.gov design system: 2-5 radio, 6-15 select, 16-100 combobox, 101+ search. GoogleChrome modern-web-guidance forms matrix: 1-5 radio, 6+ select, 10+ combobox. Queensland Gov: max 6 radios (Miller 7±2) else select. Setproduct / Cieden: 2-6 radios, 7+ dropdown. 5 is the conservative common boundary.

Note: enum AND oneOf both already produce neutral 'choices' (fieldNode + arrayNode), so the heuristic keys purely off choices.length + valueShape — no schema-keyword coupling.
close_reason: Implemented radio/checkbox-group widgets via the unified field.control 'choicegroup' archetype + option-count heuristic (OPTION_COUNT_THRESHOLD=5) in present(); wired React + vanilla (string/DOM) renderers, examples, and ADR 029; gate green.

title: Expand the default widget catalog (checkbox, radio, date, textarea, etc.)
description:
Follow-up to the wsr tracer (ADR 029). defaultPresentation + the Core widget catalog are built to GROW; this bead adds the common form controls beyond input/select/multiselect.

Two sub-cases the seam already handles:
1. SAME facts, many widgets — e.g. a boolean can be a checkbox, a Yes/No radio, a Yes/No select, or a custom push-button. Identical FieldFacts; the resolver (or a new default rule) picks the widget. This is the canonical case the present() seam was designed for.
2. NEW facts drive NEW defaults — e.g. date keys off facts.format==='date' (facts ALREADY carry format), so many are just new catalog entries + default rules with NO facts change.

Candidate archetypes: checkbox (boolean), radio group (enum, small N), textarea (string + long/format hint), date/datetime/time (format), number stepper, range/slider (min/max), color, file. Each = a neutral deriveXParts(facts) in the Core catalog (shared with the string oracle so React ≡ vanilla holds) + a defaultPresentation rule + conformance coverage.

Pure additive; do AFTER 9pb closes the dual period (present() as sole widget/parts source) OR carefully keep parser + present() in sync while the dual period is open.

title: Generalize the neutral waist to containers (NodeFacts) so present() can collapse an array/object subtree into a single widget
description:
Follow-up surfaced by the wsr tracer (ADR 029) + the VNDLY object-array multiselect case.

TODAY: FieldFacts exists only on LEAF nodes (FieldNode). A subtree array (type:array, items:object) becomes an ArrayNode with NO facts, so a PresentationResolver cannot opt it into a single collapsed widget. Leaf enum-arrays get valueShape:'array' + inline choices; subtree arrays get nothing. Object subtrees (GroupNode) likewise have no facts — this absorbs the deferred valueShape:'object' question from ADR 029.

GOAL: generalize FieldFacts into a NodeFacts waist that also projects CONTAINER nodes, so present() can collapse an array/object subtree into one leaf-like widget when a resolver asks for it.

KEY DESIGN POINTS:
- valueShape is the shape of the SUBMITTED value, independent of whether the node is currently decomposed. A leaf enum-array and a collapsed object-array BOTH get valueShape:'array' and stay consistent. Default rule keys multiselect off valueShape==='array' && choices, so a subtree array (no choices) correctly stays add/remove until a resolver overrides it.
- Multiselect-over-object-array is RICHER than the enum case: it needs (a) an option SOURCE (often async/remote), and (b) a value identity (each selection is an object {name,type} — which field is value, which is label). That is NOT 'choices'; it is 'args' ({ optionsSource, valueKey, labelKey }). Same widget name, divergent facts/args — exactly why args exists.
- Container facts likely carry valueShape + EITHER choices (finite) OR an element/item descriptor (open-ended); resolver supplies the option source via args.
- present() must be able to collapse: prune the subtree's children and emit a single control node, while submit assembly still produces the array value.

Reference (VNDLY primary multiselect pattern): allowed_criteria: { type:'array', items:{ type:'object', properties:{ name:{type:string}, type:{type:string} } } }.

Depends conceptually on the field.control facet / catalog work (9pb-adjacent) for how a collapsed container renders.

title: Close the ADR 029 dual period: parser emits facts-only, present() sole widget/parts source
description:
Follow-up to the wsr tracer (ADR 029). wsr landed the ADDITIVE seam (FieldFacts on nodes, Core present() + defaultPresentation + input/select/multiselect derivers, useSchemaForm wired through present(), conformance folded over the presented tree, golden e2e). The dual period is intentionally still open: the JSON Schema parser STILL assigns widget + builds parts in createFieldNode/arrayNode, and present(defaultPresentation) reproduces those choices identity-preservingly. So widget/parts derivation is duplicated in two places that must agree (guarded by conformance + present unit tests).

This bead closes the dual period: make the parser emit facts-only field nodes and let present() be the SOLE source of widget + parts.

Why it is its own PR (blast radius): FieldNode currently REQUIRES widget+parts. Removing parser assignment means either (a) widget/parts become non-authoritative-until-present, or (b) jsonSchemaToTree itself runs present(defaultPresentation) internally after building facts-only intermediates. Every direct `jsonSchemaToTree(schema)` -> render path (the @jsonschema-form/vanilla package, conformance, arrays/renderer/render-stability tests, ~15 files) must go through a presented tree. Doing this in the same PR that introduces present() would remove the fallback while present() is still unproven; the ADR explicitly sanctions a dual period, so we keep it one PR longer and collapse the duplication here with present() already battle-tested on main.

Scope: parser builds facts-only leaves; jsonSchemaToTree applies defaultPresentation so its return stays fully-formed for direct renders; delete buildInputAttrs/select-parts duplication from fieldNode.ts + createMultiselectFieldNode parts in arrayNode.ts; migrate redundant parser unit tests (which assert widget/parts) into present() unit tests or integration tests. Gate green throughout.

Still deferred beyond this: field.control engine/adapter rewrite; bindRenders custom-widget DX; origin rename (schema -> origin {source, schema: TOrigin}).

title: ValidationSummary should respect the showErrorsWhen display policy (ADR 027)
description: After ADR 027 flipped the default display policy to 'touched', per-field errors are gated by touched/submitted, but ValidationSummary (packages/react/src/ValidationSummary.tsx) reads useValidationIssues() directly and lists every issue that EXISTS, ungated. With live validation + default 'touched' + a mounted summary, the summary leaks all errors immediately while fields stay quiet — inconsistent UX. Decide: gate the summary on submit/touched too (probably show summary only after submit, matching its 'submit-time error summary' docstring), or make it read a policy-aware slice. Not urgent: current demos don't regress (App_09 has no live issues pre-submit; App_11 uses 'always').

title: Field-scoped (subtree) revalidation to avoid whole-document validator cost per event
description: DIRECTION (approved 2026-07-01): measure-first. ADR 023 already made re-renders O(changed-fields), so the only remaining per-event cost is the whole-document Validator (assemble + clone + AJV validate, all O(document)). Before designing any field-scoped/subtree validation (which is a big change and breaks cross-field rules), BENCHMARK the real per-event validator cost at increasing form sizes. The benchmark number is the gate: only build field-scoping if whole-document validation is genuinely hot at realistic sizes; otherwise defer with evidence (h05 collapses to at most a debounce hook + scoped issue-commit). Benchmark lives in packages/validation-ajv/bench/.

title: Native form-state: touched tracking + showErrorsWhen display policy
description: Don't show a field's error until touched/blur (RHF-style). Agnostic policy fn (issues+touched+mode -> shown paths) on the ADR 023 store + per-framework binding. Build AFTER the RHF spike reveals the seam. Boundary: native owns values+issues+touched+policy; dirty/reset/watch/async => form-lib adapter.

title: Spike: React Hook Form as the form-state layer (example/recipe, ADR 024)
description: Wire RHF over our Core tree + renderer; our Validator (ajv) as RHF resolver; touched-gated error display (mode:onTouched). Goal: prove Validator-seam reuse, discover the form-state/display seam shape, test renderer-seam expressiveness (register() injection). Example in examples/, not a package.
close_reason: Merged as PR #12 — recipe lives in examples/basic-react/src/App_12_React+ReactHookForm.tsx. Findings drove ADR 025 (impl: jsonschema-form-ml0) and notes on r1p (Standard Schema) and 8k8 (path-utils consolidation).

title: Presentation stage over neutral facts (ADR 029)
description: EPIC. Superseded framing from ADR 022. Per ADR 029: the parser produces neutral FieldFacts (label, primitive, valueShape, choices, constraints, attrs, origin) and NO widget; a dedicated pure present() stage in @jsonschema-form/core assigns widget + derives a control facet, driven by a layered PresentationResolver (shipped defaultPresentation under an optional source-agnostic consumer resolver). Core owns the built-in widget catalog (neutral derivers, shared with the string oracle for conformance); adapters bindRenders to catalog names and may extend with typed custom widgets. One unified field.control slot (no raw-vs-archetype tiers). Closed Core archetype union for typing; custom widgets branded + typed args only within the const catalog; args (not options) is the generic per-widget config bag. See architecture_records/029_presentation_stage_over_neutral_facts.md. Deferred sub-slices: bindRenders/extend custom-widget DX; the field.control engine/adapter rewrite; the origin rename.

title: Extract validator-agnostic contract suite into a shared package
description: The validator contract test suite currently lives in packages/validation-ajv/test/contract.ts and is re-exported via an awkward ./test/contract surface. With AJV + Zod (two adapters), ADR 008's second-implementation rule has fired; move it into a shared home (e.g. @jsonschema-form/validation-core or core test-utils) so adapters import it cleanly. Raised by maintainer on PR #2.
notes: implemented in PR #7 (branch feat/validation-contract-pkg) — shared @jsonschema-form/validation-contract package + ADR 020, gate-green, reviewed LGTM, in review.

title: Accessibility and validation error-display UX polish
description: Audit and improve a11y for generated forms: label/input associations, aria-invalid/describedby for errors, focus management on validation failure, and consistent error summary patterns. Includes validation error presentation polish.
notes: Implemented in GitHub PR #4 (branch feat/a11y-error-ux): error-gated aria-invalid/aria-describedby/role=alert wiring + opt-in ValidationSummary in packages/react, gate-green incl. conformance oracle, reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NITS, in review.

title: Implement @jsonschema-form/ui-tailwind Tailwind UI kit
description: Flesh out packages/ui-tailwind with Tailwind-styled default templates/widgets (inputs, labels, errors, array controls) as the first concrete UI kit on the renderer seam.

title: Rich JSON Schema typing for IoC placement autocomplete
description: Type-safe field paths and inferred data shape for JSX IoC customization (renderNode, node.child(path), parts overrides) with let-me-place-it autocomplete. Builds on type-inference prototypes jsonschema-form-gjq and jsonschema-form-nsg.
notes: implemented in PR #6 (branch feat/schema-typing-inference) — InferData<S> + FieldPath<S> compile-time typing, gate-green, in review (FieldPath being aligned to ${number} indexed paths per lead decision).

title: Implement @jsonschema-form/validation-zod adapter (second Validator implementation)
description: Add packages/validation-zod implementing the Core Validator contract as the second validation adapter (after AJV), proving the validation seam per ADR 008. Map schema constraints to Zod and integrate with the existing validation fold slot.
notes: Implemented in GitHub PR #2 (branch feat/validation-zod), gate-green, in review.

title: Submit-time validation: empty required text fields slip through (FormData sends '' so AJV 'required' passes)
React submit-time validation wiring + per-field error rendering (ADR 019 slice 2)
close_reason: Shipped: useSchemaForm { validator } + submit/errors; ValidationProvider + useFieldIssues + context-gated DefaultFieldErrors (runtime state, no IR change); AJV coerceTypes default; validation.test.tsx green; conformance unchanged; example App_09. ADR 019 slice 2.

title: Validation seam (Core Validator contract) + AJV adapter, submit-time (ADR 019)
close_reason: Shipped: Core neutral Validator contract (ValidationIssue/Result + groupIssuesByPath), @jsonschema-form/validation-ajv createAjvValidator mapping AJV errors to dot-path issues, contract-test suite proven validator-shaped against AJV + a throwaway fake. ADR 019. Gate green.

title: Revive App_07 arrays example on the new add/remove UI
description: examples/basic-react App_07_React+Arrays.tsx: dynamic arrays now render working add/remove via the continuation engine (bi4/ADR 015). Update the example to demonstrate it.
close_reason: Revived examples/basic-react App_07: rewritten to demonstrate the ADR 015 add/remove UI — dynamic object array (addresses) + string array (hobbies) with value preservation, multiselect (skills/interests), and a live submitted-JSON panel. Set App_07 as the default example. Verified live in browser: filled two addresses, removed the first, survivor kept its typed value; submit shows the collected JSON (which exposes the sparse hole addresses[0]=null — the exact case jsonschema-form-ux5 will densify).

title: Render-count perf contract for arrays via counting adapter
description: Harden the perf contract from ADR 015: a counting RendererAdapter that tallies per-node renderer invocations, asserting an unrelated parent re-render = 0 node re-renders and array add = only ArrayRoot + the new item re-render (existing items bail via memo). Today we assert DOM identity + value preservation as the proxy.
close_reason: Done: counting RendererAdapter in packages/react/src/render-counts.test.tsx pins three render-count numbers (unrelated parent re-render = 0 node renderers; add = exactly 1 removeButton/new item only; remove = 0 in survivor). Drove a real fix: per-item ArrayActionsContext value was a fresh object each ArrayRoot render, re-rendering existing items' Remove buttons through the memo-bailed NodeRenderer (caught as removeButton=2 on a single add). Stabilized via memoized per-item ArrayItemActions boundary (stable removeById + primitive id). ADR 015 decision 7 added.

title: Rebuild array add/remove UI in the continuation engine
close_reason: Array add/remove UI rebuilt on the continuation engine and demonstrated end-to-end: engine renderChild seam + EArray.renderItem, React ArrayRoot with stable monotonic keys, identical markup across React/vanilla (conformance), render-count perf contract, and the revived App_07 example (verified live). Submission correctness (dense arrays, submit-as-arrays) tracked separately as ux5/57v.

title: Rewrite governance docs to loop philosophy + IR/adapter framing
description: Rewrite README/AGENTS/CLAUDE/ARCHITECTURE to match ADRs 006-011 (Core-as-IR, schema-generates/JSX-customizes, swappability-by-2nd-impl, verification-gated loop, recursive continuation rendering, form-state shallow slot). Supersedes 'five layers', 'pair never drive', 'form library layer is primary'. Branch only.
close_reason: Rewrote README/ARCHITECTURE/AGENTS/CLAUDE to match ADRs 006-011 (Sonnet subagent, reviewed by main)

title: Array fields should always submit as arrays in form.submit()
description:
## Problem

Currently, multiselect and dynamic array fields submit as single values if only one item is selected, instead of arrays.

### Current behavior:
```typescript
// Multiselect with one selection
{ skills: 'JavaScript' }  // ❌ should be ['JavaScript']

// Multiselect with multiple selections
{ skills: ['JavaScript', 'TypeScript'] }  // ✅ correct
```

### Root cause:
In groupNode.ts submit handler, we only create arrays when FormData has duplicate keys:
```typescript
if (key in flat) {
  // Multiple values - make array
} else {
  flat[key] = value  // Single value
}
```

## Solution

1. During parsing, track which fields are arrays (multiselect, dynamic arrays)
2. In submit handler, consult schema to know which fields should be arrays
3. Always wrap those field values in arrays, even if single value

## Alternative approaches:
- Use FormData.getAll() for known array fields
- Store metadata on GroupNode about which fields are arrays
- Walk the tree to identify array fields before processing FormData

title: React: Auto-normalize form data in useSchemaForm onSubmit
description:
Once the core normalizeFormData utility exists, update useSchemaForm to automatically normalize the form data before passing it to the user's onSubmit handler.

Current: onSubmit gets raw FormEvent
Desired: onSubmit gets normalized data object

API idea:
<Form onSubmit={(data) => console.log(data)} />
// data is already { address: { street: '...', city: '...' } }

This makes the React layer more ergonomic - users don't have to manually normalize form data.

title: Core: Add utility to normalize flat FormData to nested JSON object
description:
FormData returns flat key-value pairs like { 'address.street': '123 Main', 'address.city': 'NYC' }. Need a utility function in @jsonschema-form/core to convert this back to nested JSON like { address: { street: '123 Main', city: 'NYC' } } based on the schema structure.

Should handle:
- Nested objects (dot notation paths)
- Arrays (bracket notation)
- Type coercion (numbers, booleans)
- Checkbox values
- Multi-select arrays

API idea: normalizeFormData(flatData, formNode) => nestedObject

title: Add edge case tests (deep nesting, large schemas, error handling)
Extract part builders to shared module for testability
Evaluate: Is schema type inference useful for this library?
description: After prototyping, assess tradeoffs: DX gains vs complexity, runtime vs compile-time schemas, const vs dynamic schemas. Document decision and reasoning.

title: Prototype: Infer form data shape from schema
description: Try to infer the output type of form.getData() or similar based on schema. Would this be { name: string, email: string, address: { street: string } }?

title: Prototype: Type-safe field path access (getField, walk)
description:
Experiment with inferring field paths from schema const. Can getField('address.street') be type-checked? Can walk handlers know the exact node types? ## Exploration Notes (2025-11-17) ### Four Potential Approaches: **Option A: Template Literal Types for Paths** - Extract valid paths as string literals from schema shape - getField

(path: P) - Pros: Great autocomplete, catches typos - Cons: Complex type gymnastics, breaks with runtime schemas **Option B: Generic Schema Parameter + Return Type** - parseSchema(schema: S) returns typed structure - Full type inference from schema at call site - Pros: Complete type inference - Cons: Very complex, slow compile, runtime schemas problematic **Option C: Schema Builder Pattern** - New DSL: defineSchema({ username: field.string(), ... }) - Perfect types, clean API - Pros: Best types, no inference weirdness - Cons: Can't accept arbitrary JSON Schema, bigger API surface **Option D: Hybrid - Type Inference When Possible** - Keep current runtime API - Add support for 'as const' schemas for compile-time safety - Both typed and untyped usage coexist - Pros: Backward compatible, opt-in - Cons: Two different experiences ### Open Questions: - Primary use case: static schemas (imported) or dynamic (fetched)? - Scope: Just path autocomplete, or also typed return values? - Worth the complexity? See: tmp/scratch.type-exploration.ts for code sketches

title: Explore ts-to-zod and json-schema-to-typescript libraries
description: Investigate existing TypeScript utilities for schema-to-type conversion. Can we leverage json-schema-typed better? Are there patterns we can learn from?

title: Research TanStack Form/Router type inference patterns
description: Study how TanStack achieves type inference for paths and data shapes. Document key patterns and APIs they use.

title: Explore TypeScript type inference for JSON Schema forms
description: Research and prototype how much type safety we can achieve, similar to TanStack Form/Router. Questions: Can we infer field paths? Form data shapes? Is it useful in this context? What are the tradeoffs?

title: Add date/datetime field type support
UI library layer (Tailwind, Shadcn, etc.)
Validation layer integration (AJV, Zod, Valibot)
Add textarea widget support
Extract controlIsMultiValued(control) accessor when a 2nd consumer appears
description: FieldControl models multiplicity asymmetrically, faithfully mirroring HTML: select's is a real element attribute (control.attrs.multiple, spreadable onto <select multiple>), while choicegroup's is a structural discriminator (control.multiple, already consumed into each option's input type + the role). Reads therefore differ: c.attrs.multiple vs c.multiple. Today the ONLY consumer is submit array-wrapping in groupNode.ts (keyed on control.kind + multiple, refactored off widget-name strings in the cm7 hotfix). Per ADR 008, do NOT abstract yet. Trigger: the moment a SECOND place needs 'is this control multi-valued?', extract a tiny controlIsMultiValued(control: FieldControl): boolean helper that hides the asymmetry, rather than reshaping the IR (which would duplicate select's attrs.multiple as a second source of truth).

title: Consolidate duplicate submitWith test helper
description: submitTestUtils.ts (added in PR #33) provides a shared submitWith() FormData round-trip helper, but parser.test.ts:1665-1686 still has an inline duplicate with its own globalThis.FormData mock. Import the shared helper in parser.test.ts and delete the copy so the FormData mocking behavior (incl. the try/finally restore fix) has a single source of truth.

title: Extract shared node assertion test helpers (assertArrayNode / assertGroupNode / assertField)
description: Align test narrowing with the throw-then-narrow convention already in controlTestUtils.ts (choicegroupCtl). Add test-only assertArrayNode / assertGroupNode / assertField helpers (same *TestUtils.ts exclude pattern) that throw with the node path on mismatch and narrow the type. Refactor parser.test.ts, arraysRobustness.test.ts, deepNesting.test.ts to drop 'if (node?.isArray) { ... }' conditional bodies and 'as ArrayNode' casts. Prevents the if/as antipattern from spreading as more edge-case suites are added.

title: oneOf detection too broad: only a simple const-oneOf is choices; a deep oneOf is a variant/section selector
description:
Discovered during 4j1 demo work (App_14 oneOf multiselect + App_01 country select).

BUG: the parser treats ANY oneOf as a finite set of choices:
- packages/core/src/parser/arrayNode.ts isPrimitiveArraySchema(): `if (itemsSchema.enum || itemsSchema.oneOf) return true` -> the whole array becomes a multiselect FieldNode.
- packages/core/src/parser/fieldNode.ts createFieldNode(): hasOneOf -> isSelect; buildSelectOptions() maps oneOf branches, filtering `('const' in item)`.

But oneOf is also (more commonly) a STRUCTURAL union: branches that are object subschemas (discriminated union) meant to swap out whole form sections, e.g. items.oneOf = [ {type:object, properties:{...A}}, {type:object, properties:{...B}} ]. Those branches have no `const`, so today they are FILTERED OUT -> an empty-options multiselect. The array never becomes an add/remove ArrayNode either, so the entire variant structure is silently dropped.

Only a SIMPLE oneOf should be treated as choices: every branch is a scalar constant (has `const`, primitive value), i.e. an enum-with-labels exactly like App_01 country ([{const:'US',title:'United States'}, ...]).

FIX (parser-side, front-end concern): gate the "oneOf -> choices" detection on ALL branches being simple const entries. If any branch is object/array/$ref/typed-without-const, it is NOT choices — treat it as a (currently unsupported) union/variant selector, a separate feature (discriminated-union / section-swap). At minimum, do not mis-collapse it into an empty multiselect.

Lower priority (per Tim). No consumer hits it until deep-oneOf schemas are used.

title: Simplify App_12 RHF example to standardSchemaResolver(toStandardSchema(validator))
Vanilla renderer: validation issue display (reuse agnostic issue store)
description: Vanilla DOM renderer shows validation issues by subscribing to the (lifted) issue store; the 2nd consumer that earns the form-state pkg lift. Per-framework binding + DOM tests.

title: Lift issueStore to a shared framework-agnostic form-state package
description: createIssueStore is pure JS (no React) but lives in packages/react. Per ADR 008, lift to a shared @jsonschema-form/form-state pkg when a 2nd consumer (vanilla error display) needs it. Trivial move, no React deps.

title: vanilla-dom: join cross-framework conformance + extract shared renderAttrs/escape helpers + add missing parity schemas
description: Follow-ups from PR #5 review: wire renderToDom into conformance.test.tsx as a third participant; extract shared renderAttrs/escapeText/escapeAttr between renderToString and domRenderer; add the missing parity cases (nameless nested group; select+multiselect+description); add DOM renderNode/continuation/diagnostic-floor tests.

title: Standalone (non-React) example/playground for the vanilla DOM renderer
description: examples/basic-react is React-only; add a plain-HTML example page that mounts the createDomRenderer() output so the DOM adapter is demoable. Raised during PR #5 review.

title: Assert a11y/error-state aria wiring via the cross-framework conformance oracle
description: a11y error wiring tests are currently React-adapter-specific (a11y-errors.test.tsx). Once a second adapter renders error state, assert error-derived aria attrs once in the cross-framework conformance oracle (attrs can be Core-specified per ADR 012) instead of per-framework. Raised by maintainer on PR #4.

title: Normalize validator keyword vocabulary across backends
description: AJV emits JSON-Schema keywords (minLength, required) while the Zod adapter emits Zod codes (too_small, invalid_type) at the same dot-paths; ValidationIssue.keyword is therefore backend-specific. Consider a normalization helper or an ADR so keyword-driven error UIs are backend-agnostic. Paths + messages are already portable.

title: vanilla-dom-adapter: real DOM renderer (not HTML strings)
description: Add a renderer adapter that constructs actual DOM elements (document.createElement) instead of emitting HTML strings from defaultAdapter. Enables imperative DOM frameworks and safer escaping by default.
notes: Implemented in GitHub PR #5 (branch feat/vanilla-dom-renderer): static real-DOM RendererAdapter in packages/vanilla, gate-green, reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NITS, in review. Scope is static rendering; array interactivity is a follow-up.

title: Validation: field/group-scoped validation triggers
description: Allow validating a single field, subtree, or section on demand (blur, section next, partial submit) instead of whole-form validation only. Likely depends on a reactive form-state slot to re-render scoped errors.

title: Validation: async validation support
description: Support async validators (remote checks, debounced server validation) wired through the Validator seam and rendered in field error slots. Likely depends on a reactive form-state slot for pending/success/error states.

title: Validation: live/reactive validation (on change/blur)
description: Validate fields as the user edits (change/blur), surfacing errors inline before submit. Likely depends on a reactive form-state slot (RHF/TanStack or similar) rather than submit-only native FormData.
notes: Reactive (on-change) validation underway in branch feat/reactive-validation + ADR 021 (validate-on-change via the Validator seam reading native FormData).

title: JSON Schema: format and pattern constraint coverage
description: Broaden Core/React widget + validation mapping for format (email, uri, etc.) and pattern regex constraints. Note: date/datetime widgets tracked separately in jsonschema-form-672.

title: JSON Schema: tuple arrays (prefixItems)
description: Support JSON Schema tuple form: prefixItems (and items tuple semantics) as fixed-length array rows with per-index item schemas, not homogeneous dynamic arrays.

title: JSON Schema: array-of-primitives widgets
description: First-class support for arrays whose items are primitives (string/number/boolean) with add/remove UX. Related: jsonschema-form-kon (tags widget) and jsonschema-form-ci4 (nested primitive arrays) cover narrower cases.

title: JSON Schema: const fields (read-only/hidden fixed values)
description: Support const schemas as fixed-value fields (hidden input or read-only display) included in submit data. Distinct from enum/oneOf selection UX.

title: JSON Schema: default value prefill
description: Honor schema default keywords by pre-populating field initial values (native FormData defaults and/or reactive form-state slot). Includes nested defaults and array item defaults where applicable.

title: JSON Schema: if/then/else conditional fields
description: Support if/then/else (and dependentSchemas-style conditionals) by showing/hiding or enabling branch fields based on current form values during render/state updates.

title: JSON Schema: anyOf/oneOf conditional subforms
description: Compile anyOf/oneOf into selectable subforms (tabs/radio/select + nested group) so users pick a variant and fill the matching branch. oneOf should enforce exactly-one selection semantics.

title: JSON Schema: local $ref/$defs/definitions resolution
description: Core jsonSchemaToTree should resolve local $ref pointers against $defs/definitions (and sibling definitions) before building the form tree. Covers internal references only; external/remote refs are out of scope unless added later.
notes: Implemented in GitHub PR #3 (branch feat/schema-ref-resolution), gate-green, in review.

title: Refactor node types to be inferred from factory functions
Support false values for unchecked checkboxes in form.submit()
description:
## Current Behavior

Unchecked checkboxes don't appear in FormData at all, so they're omitted from submit data:

```typescript
// Checkbox unchecked → not in FormData → not in submit data
{ name: 'John' }
// Checkbox checked → 'on' in FormData → true in submit data
{ name: 'John', subscribe: true }
```

## Problem

Can't distinguish between:
- Field not present in form (e.g., conditional field)
- Field present but unchecked (user explicitly declined)

## Proposed Solution

Add hidden input with value="false" before each checkbox:
```typescript
// In FieldParts for checkboxes
parts.hiddenFalse = {
  attrs: { type: 'hidden', name: path, value: 'false' }
}
```

When rendered:
```html
<input type="hidden" name="subscribe" value="false" />
<input type="checkbox" name="subscribe" value="on" />
```

FormData behavior:
- Unchecked: only hidden submits → "false" → transform to false
- Checked: checkbox overwrites hidden → "on" → transform to true

## Alternative: Configuration

Add option to form.submit():
```typescript
form.submit(onSubmit, { 
  omitUncheckedCheckboxes: false  // default: true for backwards compat
})
```

## Related

- Rails and other frameworks use this hidden input pattern
- Ensures checkboxes always submit a value

title: Support nested primitive arrays
description:
## Current Behavior

Nested primitive arrays (e.g., array of multiselect fields) are explicitly unsupported and throw an error:

```typescript
// In arrayNode.ts:187-189
if (childArray.nodeType !== 'array') {
  throw new Error('Nested primitive arrays not yet supported')
}
```

## Use Case

Schema with array of arrays where inner arrays have enum/oneOf:

```json
{
  "type": "array",
  "items": {
    "type": "array",
    "items": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["red", "blue", "green"]
    }
  }
}
```

This would represent something like "multiple color palettes, each with multiple colors".

## Implementation

1. Allow ArrayItemNode to contain multiselect FieldNode as child
2. Update createArrayItemNode() to handle when createArrayNode() returns FieldNode (multiselect)
3. Add tests for nested primitive arrays
4. Consider UI/UX for nested multiselects (e.g., each item shows a multiselect dropdown)

## Related

- Works with complex nested arrays (objects containing arrays)
- Only fails for primitive arrays nested in arrays

title: Add tags/pills widget for freeform array inputs
description:
## Use Case

Arrays of strings without enum (e.g., hobbies, email addresses) currently use add/remove buttons with text inputs. A tags/pills UI would be more user-friendly.

## Proposed UX

Similar to Gmail's email composer:
- Input field at the bottom
- Type value and press Enter/comma to add as a pill/badge
- Click X on pill to remove
- Pills display above input field

## Implementation Options

1. New widget type: widget: 'tags' (auto-detect for string arrays without enum)
2. Schema hint: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, 'ui:widget': 'tags' }
3. Custom template override via useSchemaForm options

## Related

- Could be added to @jsonschema-form/ui-tailwind package
- Consider supporting suggestions/autocomplete from previous values

title: Configure sparse array handling in form.submit()
description: Currently, form.submit() unflatten creates sparse arrays when array indices are non-consecutive (e.g., hobbies.0, hobbies.2 → [item0, , item2]). Some users may want to compact arrays by removing gaps. Add configuration option: form.submit(onSubmit, { compactArrays: false }). Default to sparse (current behavior) to match HTML form semantics.

title: Modular form section data extraction
Configurable value omission in form submission
Switch to TypeScript composite mode for package builds
Clean up stub packages or document roadmap
Add stricter TypeScript compiler flags
Replace magic strings with widget type constants
Schema resolution support (, allOf, anyOf, oneOf)
JSON Schema: allOf merge during compilation
description: Support allOf by merging subschemas into a single effective schema/node during jsonSchemaToTree compilation. Distinct from anyOf/oneOf subform selection.
notes:
implemented in PR #8 (branch feat/schema-allof) — allOf object-composition merge, gate-green, reviewed APPROVE-WITH-NITS, in review.
REJECTED: constraint-MERGING approach (PR #8). Validation stays the side-loaded validator's job; live constraints come from reactive validation (ADR 021). Rework allOf as RENDER-ONLY structural composition (union of properties) with a demo. Demoted/deferred behind reactive-validation (jsonschema-form-e1e) and widget-selection (jsonschema-form-p5l / ADR 022) threads.

value: Never pipe 'npm run gate' (or any test command) through tail/head — the shell returns the pipe's LAST command exit code (tail=0), masking vitest's non-zero. This is exactly how cm7 merged with 6 red React tests. Capture the real exit code: 'npm run gate > /tmp/gate.log 2>&1; echo EXIT=$?'. Also: the React package DOES run in the gate (chromium), it just runs early (alphabetical) so tail shows only the last package.
